questions.question: What was a major milestone achieved in the 2020s for quantum repeaters?
questions.explanation: In the early 2020s, multi-node quantum networks with memory (like the Delft network) demonstrated entanglement distribution with memory storage and basic network operations, marking significant progress toward practical repeaters.

questions.question: Which platform offers the best combination of memory coherence and gate fidelity?
questions.explanation: Trapped ions offer exceptional coherence times (seconds to minutes) combined with the highest gate fidelities (>99.9%). The main challenge is efficient photon collection for networking.

questions.question: What is a major advantage of NV centers for repeater implementation?

options.text: Good photon-matter interface at optical wavelengths

questions.explanation: NV centers offer a good interface between electron spin qubits and optical photons, enabling heralded entanglement generation. They operate at ~4K and have demonstrated multi-node networking.

questions.question: What is a key challenge for deploying quantum repeaters in the field?
questions.explanation: Most leading platforms require cryogenic temperatures (4K or lower), which is challenging for field deployment. Providing reliable cryogenic infrastructure at remote repeater stations adds significant complexity and cost.

questions.question: What is the typical segment length used in current repeater designs?
questions.explanation: Current designs typically use 20-50 km segments, balancing fiber loss (manageable at these distances) against the number of swapping operations needed. This matches the capabilities of current memory technologies.

questions.question: When are first commercial quantum repeaters expected to be deployed?
questions.explanation: Based on current progress, first commercial quantum repeater deployments are expected in the late 2020s to early 2030s. This timeline allows for continued technology development and systems integration.

questions.question: All leading quantum memory platforms operate at room temperature.
questions.explanation: False. Most leading platforms require cryogenic cooling: NV centers at ~4K, trapped ions at mK, neutral atoms at μK. Only atomic vapor cells operate near room temperature, but with shorter coherence times.
